threats.title: Uninitialized Memory Access in getRewards Causes Incorrect Slashing of All Stakers
threats.hypothesis_description: The `getRewards` function in `SurplusGuildMinter` evaluates the `slashed` condition against an uninitialized memory struct before loading the actual user stake from storage. This causes any user staking on a term with a historical loss to be incorrectly flagged as slashed, permanently destroying their stake.
threats.investigation_rationale: The `slashed` boolean is set if `lastGaugeLoss > uint256(userStake.lastGaugeLoss)`. Because `userStake` is a named return variable, it is zero-initialized at this point. If the term has ever experienced a loss (`lastGaugeLoss > 0`), `slashed` becomes true. The actual stake is loaded on the next line. Later in the function, if `slashed` is true, the user's entire stake is zeroed out, permanently locking their CREDIT in the surplus buffer.
preconditions: A lending term has experienced at least one gauge loss (`lastGaugeLoss > 0`)
A user has an active stake in that term via `SurplusGuildMinter`

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: State evaluation using uninitialized return variables
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: None - anyone can call `getRewards` on any user
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: Any time after a term experiences a loss

operational_questions: Can an attacker call `getRewards` on all stakers to wipe their positions?
Are the underlying CREDIT tokens permanently locked in the ProfitManager?
validation_requirements: Deploy a term, simulate a loss, stake as a new user, and call `getRewards`
Verify that the user's stake is zeroed and funds are unrecoverable

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Identify a lending term that has previously suffered a gauge loss.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Observe users staking CREDIT into this term via `SurplusGuildMinter`.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: The contract incorrectly evaluates them as slashed, zeroes their stake, and locks their CREDIT.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Complete loss of user funds staked in affected terms
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - requires governance intervention to manually refund locked CREDIT

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Unexpected `Unstake` events with 0 user stake remaining
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Users unable to unstake their CREDIT
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Third-party addresses calling `getRewards` on behalf of others

threats.title: Debt Ceiling Utilization Prevents Slashing of Malicious Gauges
threats.hypothesis_description: The `applyGaugeLoss` function slashes voters by decrementing their gauge weight. However, `_decrementGaugeWeight` reverts if the term's issuance exceeds the new debt ceiling. If a term is fully utilized, slashing voters is impossible, protecting malicious voters and locking their tokens.
threats.investigation_rationale: When bad debt occurs, the term's issuance remains high. To slash a voter, `applyGaugeLoss` calls `_decrementGaugeWeight`, which calculates `debtCeilingAfterDecrement`. If the removed weight drops the ceiling below current issuance, the transaction reverts with 'GuildToken: debt ceiling used'. This creates a deadlock where voters cannot be slashed, and innocent voters cannot transfer their GUILD tokens because freeing weight reverts.

proof_of_signal.pattern: `require(issuance <= debtCeilingAfterDecrement, "GuildToken: debt ceiling used");` inside weight decrement hook

preconditions: A lending term has active loans utilizing its debt ceiling
A loss is notified on the term

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Slashing mechanism depends on external lending term utilization state
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: While term issuance exceeds the post-slash debt ceiling

operational_questions: Can borrowers intentionally max out issuance to protect voters from slashing?
Does this permanently lock GUILD tokens for all voters of the term?
validation_requirements: Simulate a fully utilized term with a notified loss
Attempt to call `applyGaugeLoss` and observe the revert
Attempt to transfer GUILD tokens as an innocent voter and observe the revert

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Attacker votes for a term and borrows up to the maximum debt ceiling.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Attacker intentionally defaults, causing a gauge loss.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Anyone attempting to call `applyGaugeLoss` to slash the attacker fails because issuance > new debt ceiling.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Attacker's GUILD tokens are protected from slashing until loans are closed, and innocent users are locked.
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Capital to borrow up to the debt ceiling

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Core slashing mechanism bypassed; GUILD token transfers globally disrupted for affected users
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - requires borrowers to repay or auctions to conclude to lower issuance
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: Slashing, GUILD transfers, Gauge weight management

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Failed `applyGaugeLoss` transactions with 'debt ceiling used' revert reason

threats.title: Infinite Loop in _decrementWeightUntilFree via Zero-Weight Gauges
threats.hypothesis_description: The `_decrementWeightUntilFree` function in `ERC20Gauges` contains an infinite loop if a user has a gauge with zero weight. This can be exploited to permanently brick the `SurplusGuildMinter` contract or DoS individual users.
threats.investigation_rationale: In `_decrementWeightUntilFree`, the loop variable `i` is incremented inside an `if (userGaugeWeight != 0)` block. If a gauge has 0 weight, `i` never increments, causing an Out-Of-Gas revert. An attacker can force `SurplusGuildMinter` to add a 0-weight gauge by calling `stake` when `mintRatio` is 0, or with a small amount that rounds to 0. Subsequent unstakes will trigger the infinite loop, locking all funds in the minter.
preconditions: A user or contract has a gauge in their `_userGauges` set with a weight of 0
The user attempts to transfer or burn tokens, triggering `_decrementWeightUntilFree`

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Loop increment logic nested inside conditional block

operational_questions: Can an attacker reliably inject a 0-weight gauge into a victim contract's state?
Does `SurplusGuildMinter.stake` allow 0-weight gauge additions if `mintRatio` is 0?
validation_requirements: Call `incrementGauge(gauge, 0)` as a user, then attempt to transfer tokens
Verify the transaction reverts with Out-Of-Gas

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Governance sets `mintRatio` to 0 (or attacker finds a rounding vulnerability).
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: SGM now has a 0-weight gauge. Any user calling `unstake` triggers `GuildToken.burn`, which calls `_decrementWeightUntilFree`, hitting the infinite loop and locking all SGM funds.
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Requires specific `mintRatio` configuration or rounding exploitation

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Permanent lock of all funds in SurplusGuildMinter or targeted user wallets
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - requires contract upgrades or complex state manipulation to clear the 0-weight gauge

execution_requirements_analysis.technical_skills: Understanding of EVM gas limits and loop constructs
execution_requirements_analysis.operational_timing: Exploitable immediately if conditions are met

investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Users unable to move GUILD tokens

threats.title: Division by Zero in debtCeiling Locks GUILD Tokens After Term Offboarding
threats.hypothesis_description: When the last active gauge of a specific type is offboarded, `totalTypeWeight` becomes 0. However, `getGaugeWeight` for the deprecated gauge remains > 0. Subsequent calls to `debtCeiling()` on the deprecated gauge will divide by 0, reverting the transaction and preventing users from unvoting.
threats.investigation_rationale: In `GuildToken.removeGauge`, the gauge's weight is subtracted from `totalTypeWeight`, but `getGaugeWeight[gauge]` is preserved. If `totalTypeWeight` reaches 0, `LendingTerm.debtCeiling()` will execute `(totalBorrowedCredit * toleratedGaugeWeight) / totalWeight`, where `totalWeight` is 0. Because `GuildToken._decrementGaugeWeight` calls `debtCeiling()` to check utilization, the division by zero prevents any user from removing their weight from the deprecated gauge, permanently locking their GUILD tokens.

proof_of_signal.pattern: `totalWeight` can be 0 while `gaugeWeight` > 0, leading to `debtCeilingBefore = (...) / totalWeight`

preconditions: A lending term is offboarded (deprecated)
It was the only active term of its `gaugeType`, making `totalTypeWeight` 0
The term has non-zero `issuance`

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Preservation of individual gauge weights while zeroing aggregate weights
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires term offboarding (governance or public poll)
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: After offboarding until all loans are closed

operational_questions: Does `debtCeiling()` reliably revert when `totalTypeWeight` is 0?
Does this revert propagate to `_decrementGaugeWeight` and lock user tokens?
validation_requirements: Deploy a single term of a specific type, vote for it, and borrow
Offboard the term
Attempt to call `decrementGauge` and observe the division by zero revert

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Identify a gauge type with only one active term.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Trigger the offboarding of this term via `LendingTermOffboarding`.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: All users who voted for this term are unable to unvote or transfer their GUILD tokens due to the division by zero in `debtCeiling`.
preliminary_attack_methodology.coordination_requirements: Ability to pass an offboarding poll

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Permanent lock of GUILD tokens for all voters of the offboarded term
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - requires all borrowers to repay their loans to bypass the `issuance != 0` check
potential_operational_impact.affected_operations: GUILD token transfers, Gauge weight management

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Failed `decrementGauge` transactions with division by zero errors
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Users unable to unvote from deprecated terms
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: Targeted offboarding of isolated gauge types

threats.title: Mass Exit of Rebasing Supply Causes Overflow and Traps Remaining Users
threats.hypothesis_description: In `ERC20RebaseDistributor`, if a massive portion of the rebasing supply exits, the `updateTotalRebasingShares` function recalculates the target share price. If the `percentChange` is extremely small, the calculation can overflow `uint224`, reverting the transaction and trapping the remaining rebasing users.
threats.investigation_rationale: When shares decrease, `percentChange = (sharesAfter * 1e30) / sharesBefore`. If `sharesAfter` is tiny compared to `sharesBefore` (e.g., a whale exits leaving only dust), `percentChange` becomes very small. The new target share price is `currentRebasingSharePrice + (delta * 1e30) / percentChange`. Dividing by a tiny `percentChange` results in a massive number that exceeds `type(uint224).max`. The `SafeCastLib.safeCastTo224` call will revert, preventing the whale from exiting or trapping the remaining users.
preconditions: A distribution has occurred, setting a higher target share price (`delta > 0`)
A user holding the vast majority of rebasing shares attempts to exit or transfer

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Share price recalculation scales inversely with remaining shares

operational_questions: What is the exact ratio of `sharesBefore` to `sharesAfter` required to trigger the overflow?
Can an attacker intentionally create this state to trap other users?
validation_requirements: Simulate a state with 1,000,000 rebasing shares
Trigger a distribution
Attempt to exit 999,999 shares and observe the `safeCastTo224` revert

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Attacker enters rebase with a massive amount of CREDIT.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: A distribution occurs, creating a pending share price increase.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Attacker attempts to exit almost all their shares, leaving a tiny fraction.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: The transaction reverts due to overflow, preventing the attacker from exiting, OR the attacker exits in chunks, eventually pushing the state to a point where the last users cannot exit.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Rebasing users permanently unable to exit or transfer their CREDIT tokens
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - requires contract upgrade to fix the math

execution_requirements_analysis.technical_skills: Understanding of share math and EVM limits

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Failed `exitRebase` or `transfer` transactions with SafeCast reverts
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: Users unable to move CREDIT tokens

threats.title: Re-onboarding Without Cleanup Causes Permanent PSM Redemption Pause
threats.hypothesis_description: If a lending term is offboarded and then re-onboarded before `cleanup()` is called, the `canOffboard` flag remains true. Anyone can immediately call `offboard()` again, incrementing `nOffboardingsInProgress` a second time. Since `cleanup()` only decrements it once, the counter permanently remains > 0, freezing all PSM redemptions.
threats.investigation_rationale: The `offboard` function increments `nOffboardingsInProgress` and pauses the PSM if it was 0. The `cleanup` function decrements it and unpauses the PSM if it reaches 0, while also setting `canOffboard = false`. If a term is re-onboarded (via governance) while `canOffboard` is still true, an attacker can call `offboard` again. This double-increments the counter. When `cleanup` is finally called, it only decrements once and clears the flag, leaving a permanent residual value in `nOffboardingsInProgress`.
preconditions: A term is successfully offboarded via polling (`canOffboard` = true)
Governance re-onboards the term before `cleanup` is called (e.g., because loans are still open)

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: State flag `canOffboard` persists across lifecycle resets
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: Requires governance to re-onboard the term

operational_questions: Is it operationally expected to re-onboard terms that were previously offboarded?
Can `nOffboardingsInProgress` be manually reset by governance?
validation_requirements: Offboard a term, re-onboard it via governance, and call `offboard` again
Call `cleanup` and verify that `nOffboardingsInProgress` remains > 0
Verify that `SimplePSM.redeem` reverts

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Wait for governance to re-onboard a previously offboarded term that hasn't been cleaned up.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: The term is offboarded again, and `nOffboardingsInProgress` is double-incremented.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Even after all loans close and `cleanup` is called, PSM redemptions remain permanently paused.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Permanent freeze of the Peg Stability Module (PSM) redemptions
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - requires contract upgrade or emergency action to manually reset the counter

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Multiple `Offboard` events for the same term without intervening `Cleanup` events

threats.title: Catastrophic Loss Causes Underflow in creditMultiplier Calculation
threats.hypothesis_description: In `ProfitManager.notifyPnL`, if a loan defaults and the resulting loss exceeds the remaining `creditTotalSupply`, the `creditMultiplier` calculation underflows and reverts. This prevents the bad debt from being processed and permanently locks the auction.
threats.investigation_rationale: The formula `newCreditMultiplier = (creditMultiplier * (creditTotalSupply - loss)) / creditTotalSupply` assumes `loss <= creditTotalSupply`. If `creditMultiplier` has already dropped significantly due to prior losses, the `principal` of older loans (calculated as `borrowAmount * borrowCreditMultiplier / currentCreditMultiplier`) is artificially inflated in terms of current CREDIT. A default on such a loan can produce a `loss` that exceeds the current total supply, causing an underflow. The `notifyPnL` function reverts, meaning `AuctionHouse.bid` or `LendingTerm.forgive` cannot complete.

proof_of_signal.pattern: Subtraction `creditTotalSupply - loss` without checking if `loss > creditTotalSupply`

preconditions: The protocol has suffered significant prior losses, lowering the `creditMultiplier`
A large loan opened at a higher multiplier defaults
The calculated `loss` exceeds `CreditToken.totalSupply()`

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Unbounded subtraction in core economic math

operational_questions: Can a malicious borrower intentionally create this scenario to lock their collateral?
What happens to the protocol if a loss cannot be processed?
validation_requirements: Simulate a scenario where `creditMultiplier` drops to 0.1
Default a loan that was opened at 1.0
Verify that `notifyPnL` reverts with an underflow

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Market conditions cause cascading liquidations and lower the `creditMultiplier`.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: The auction concludes, but `onBid` reverts because `notifyPnL` underflows.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: The loan remains permanently stuck in the auction phase, and bad debt is never realized.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Auctions permanently bricked; bad debt accounting broken
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - requires protocol upgrades to handle the underflow

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Failed `bid` or `forgive` transactions with arithmetic underflow errors

threats.title: Profit Distributed to Zero-Weight Gauges is Permanently Locked
threats.hypothesis_description: If a lending term generates profit but its gauge weight is 0, the `amountForGuild` portion of the profit is calculated and subtracted from the total, but the `gaugeProfitIndex` is not updated. These CREDIT tokens remain permanently stuck in the `ProfitManager`.
threats.investigation_rationale: In `notifyPnL`, the profit is split into `amountForSurplusBuffer`, `amountForGuild`, `amountForOther`, and `amountForCredit`. If `_gaugeWeight == 0`, the code skips updating `gaugeProfitIndex[gauge]`. However, `amountForGuild` was already subtracted from the pool of funds distributed to `amountForCredit`. Because the index isn't updated, no GUILD voter can claim these tokens, and there is no fallback mechanism to sweep them.

proof_of_signal.pattern: `if (_gaugeWeight != 0)` block skips index update, but `amountForGuild` was already allocated

preconditions: A lending term has active loans generating interest
All GUILD voters remove their weight from the term, or the term is offboarded (weight becomes 0)
A loan is repaid, notifying profit

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Conditional state update without reallocating unused funds
initial_operational_evidence.exploitation_window: When a term's weight drops to 0 while loans are active

operational_questions: How much profit is expected to be lost during term offboarding?
Can attackers intentionally unvote before repayment to burn yield?
validation_requirements: Vote for a term, borrow, then unvote (weight = 0)
Repay the loan
Verify that `amountForGuild` CREDIT tokens are stuck in `ProfitManager`

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Attacker observes a large repayment pending on a term.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: If the term has low weight, attacker flash-loans GUILD, votes, and unvotes to manipulate weight (if possible), or simply waits for natural weight decay.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: Guild's share of the profit is permanently locked instead of going to lenders.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Permanent loss of yield for the protocol
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium - requires a new governance function to sweep stuck tokens

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: Increasing CREDIT balance in `ProfitManager` beyond surplus buffer requirements

threats.title: Discontinuous Auction End State Forces Total Loss
threats.hypothesis_description: At the exact moment an auction expires (`startTime + auctionDuration`), `creditAsked` drops to 0. The `bid()` function reverts if `creditAsked == 0`, forcing the use of `forgive()`, which marks the loan as a 100% loss even if the collateral retains full value.
threats.investigation_rationale: In `AuctionHouse.getBidDetail`, if `block.timestamp >= _startTime + auctionDuration`, `creditAsked` is implicitly 0. The `bid` function requires `creditAsked != 0`. If network congestion, sequencer downtime, or MEV censorship prevents bids until the exact expiration second, legitimate bidders are locked out. The only resolution is `forgive()`, which socializes a total loss to the protocol and leaves the collateral stuck in the lending term.
preconditions: An auction reaches its maximum duration without being bid on

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: Discontinuous state change at boundary condition

operational_questions: Can an attacker censor bids to force a total loss?
How is the stuck collateral recovered?
validation_requirements: Start an auction and warp time to `startTime + auctionDuration`
Attempt to call `bid` and observe the revert
Call `forgive` and verify the protocol takes a 100% loss

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Attacker identifies a highly valuable liquidation.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Attacker uses MEV or network congestion to censor bids until the auction expires.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: The protocol is forced to take a total loss via `forgive`, and the attacker potentially buys the collateral cheaply via a separate governance proposal later.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Unnecessary socialization of bad debt; collateral stuck
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: Medium - requires governance `emergencyAction` to extract collateral

threats.title: forgive() on Active Auction Permanently Bricks AuctionHouse
threats.hypothesis_description: If governance calls `forgive()` on a loan that is currently in an active auction, the loan is closed, but the auction remains active. The auction can never be settled, permanently inflating `nAuctionsInProgress` and blocking `setAuctionHouse`.
threats.investigation_rationale: The `forgive()` function sets `loans[loanId].closeTime = block.timestamp` but does not check if `callTime != 0` (i.e., the loan is in auction). If an auction is active, `AuctionHouse.bid()` or `AuctionHouse.forgive()` will eventually call `LendingTerm.onBid()`. `onBid` requires `closeTime == 0`, so it reverts. The auction state is permanently stuck, and `nAuctionsInProgress` never decrements. `setAuctionHouse` requires `nAuctionsInProgress == 0`, so the auction house can never be upgraded.

proof_of_signal.pattern: `forgive()` sets `closeTime` without checking or clearing auction state

preconditions: A loan is called and an auction is started
Governance calls `forgive()` on the loan before the auction concludes

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: State transition bypasses expected lifecycle checks

operational_questions: Is governance expected to use `forgive` on active auctions?
Can `nAuctionsInProgress` be manually corrected?
validation_requirements: Start an auction
Call `forgive` as governor
Attempt to call `bid` and observe the revert
Verify `setAuctionHouse` reverts

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: An operational emergency occurs, and governance decides to forgive a loan that is currently in auction.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: The loan closes, but the auction is orphaned.
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_4: The protocol is permanently unable to upgrade the `AuctionHouse`.

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: Inability to upgrade core protocol components (`AuctionHouse`)
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: High - requires `emergencyAction` to manually edit storage slots
